---
title: "Module 10: Corporate AI Deployment"
subtitle: "MGMT 675: Generative AI for Finance"
author: "Kerry Back, Rice University"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, revealjs-style.scss]
    slide-number: c
    transition: fade
    navigation-mode: linear
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    margin: 0.05
    center: true
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script src="https://hypothes.is/embed.js" async></script>
---


## Where AI Can Live {.section-divider}

## The Deployment Spectrum

We discussed data security risks with the Deloitte Canada case. The key question is: [where does inference happen]{.amber}?

:::{.three-cards}
:::{.card .card-light}
[**Public Cloud API**]{.card-title}

- Research, drafting, coding assistance
- Non-sensitive data analysis
- Frontier model quality
- No infrastructure to manage
:::

:::{.card .card-light}
[**Cloud-Hosted Private AI**]{.card-title}

- AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, GCP Vertex AI
- Models run in your VPC with private endpoints
- Customer-managed encryption keys
- Meets HIPAA, SOX, FedRAMP for many use cases
:::

:::{.card .card-light}
[**On-Prem / Private Cloud**]{.card-title}

- Air-gapped or classified environments
- Data that cannot transit any external network
- Full physical control of hardware
- Highest cost, highest data control
:::
:::

:::{.explainer style="text-align:center;"}
Cloud-hosted private AI (middle column) is the [most common enterprise pattern]{.amber} today. For most of your work, public cloud APIs are fine --- just know when they're not.
:::

## Agents Can't Grade Their Own Work

A recent Anthropic engineering post makes a critical point about AI agents:

> "When asked to evaluate work they've produced, agents tend to respond by confidently praising the work --- even when, to a human observer, the quality is obviously mediocre."

The solution: a [generator-evaluator separation]{.amber} --- use a separate agent to critique.

:::{.two-cards}
:::{.card .card-light}
[**The Pattern**]{.card-title}

- A **generator** agent writes the code
- A separate **evaluator** agent tests and critiques it
- The evaluator is tuned to be [skeptical]{.amber}
- They iterate via "sprint contracts" with success criteria defined before implementation
:::

:::{.card .card-dark}
[**Why It Works**]{.card-title}

- "Tuning a standalone evaluator to be skeptical turns out to be far more tractable than making a generator critical of its own work"
- The evaluator catches real bugs: broken logic, API errors, incomplete features
- Same idea as [makers and checkers]{.amber} (Module 7)
:::
:::

:::{.explainer style="text-align:center;"}
Source: Prithvi Rajasekaran, "[Harness design for long-running application development](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/harness-design-long-running-apps)," Anthropic Labs, March 2026.
:::

## Running AI Locally {.section-divider}

## Ollama: Local LLMs in One Installer

[Ollama](https://ollama.com) lets you run open-source models on your laptop --- no cloud, no API key, no data leaving your machine.

:::{.two-col}
::::{.column}
**How it works**

1. Download and run the Ollama installer
2. Run a model: `ollama run gemma4:e2b`
3. Chat locally in the terminal
4. All inference runs on your CPU/GPU
::::

::::{.column}
**Model sizes and RAM**

- **Gemma 4 E2B** (Google, ~2B active) --- runs on [8GB RAM]{.amber}
- **Gemma 4 E4B** (Google, ~4B active) --- runs on 8GB RAM
- **Llama 3.1 8B** (Meta) --- needs 16GB RAM
- Models download once and run offline
::::
:::

## Exercise: Running a Local Model

:::{.two-col}
::::{.column}
**Setup**

1. Go to [ollama.com](https://ollama.com) and download the installer for your OS
2. Run the installer (no terminal needed)
3. Once installed, open a terminal and run:
   ```
   ollama run gemma4:e2b
   ```
4. Then try:
   ```
   ollama run gemma4:e4b
   ```
::::

::::{.column}
**The prompt**

Give each model this prompt and compare their responses:

> *"A private equity firm is evaluating a manufacturing company with $50M in revenue, 12% EBITDA margins, and 8% annual revenue growth. Comparable transactions have been done at 7--9x EBITDA. What is a reasonable valuation range, and what are the top three risks you would flag before proceeding?"*

**What to notice**: quality of reasoning, how each model handles uncertainty, and whether either refuses or hedges differently.
::::
:::

[Both models run entirely on your machine. No data is sent anywhere.]{.amber}

## AI Adoption Roadmap {.section-divider}

## The Sandbox-Audit-Deploy Pattern

Before deploying any AI workflow to your team, follow three phases.

:::{.step-flow}
:::{.step-card}
:::{.step-icon}
🧪
:::
:::{.step-title}
1. Sandbox
:::
:::{.step-desc}
Test with dummy data
:::
:::

:::{.step-card}
:::{.step-icon}
🔍
:::
:::{.step-title}
2. Audit
:::
:::{.step-desc}
Review logs and logic
:::
:::

:::{.step-card}
:::{.step-icon}
🚀
:::
:::{.step-title}
3. Deploy
:::
:::{.step-desc}
Roll out with monitoring
:::
:::
:::

Test with synthetic data, review logs and logic, then roll out with weekly monitoring.

## Adoption Timeline: From Quick Wins to Strategic

Each adoption stage maps to a trust level.

1. **Quick wins** (weeks) --- *light verification*:
   - Chat for research, summarization, drafts
   - Upload spreadsheets for ad-hoc analysis

2. **Medium-term** (months) --- *test-before-deploy*:
   - Skills for recurring workflows
   - MCP integrations and web apps

3. **Strategic** (quarters) --- *full audit trail*:
   - Production apps with authentication
   - AI usage policy and governance framework

## Summary {.section-divider}

## Summary

:::{.three-cards}
:::{.card .card-light}
[**Where AI Lives**]{.card-title}

- Public cloud → VPC-hosted → on-prem
- Match deployment to data sensitivity
- Most work: cloud APIs are fine
:::

:::{.card .card-light}
[**Working with Agents**]{.card-title}

- Agents can't grade their own work
- Generator-evaluator separation
- Local models via Ollama for sensitive data
:::

:::{.card .card-light}
[**Adoption**]{.card-title}

- Sandbox → audit → deploy
- Quick wins → medium-term → strategic
- Trust is the bottleneck
:::
:::

:::{style="text-align:center; margin-top:1em;"}
[Know where AI can live, use the right patterns, adopt in stages.]{.amber}
:::
